function isCyclicRotation(B, T) {
    const k = B.length;
    if (k === 0) return true;

    // Using a separator ensures that multi-digit numbers don't merge (e.g., [1, 23] != [12, 3])
    const B_str = B.join(',');
    const T_str = T.join(',');

    // If T is a rotation of B, T must appear in B+B.
    // Since we use delimiters, let's ensure the doubled string maintains the structure.
    
    // If B = [1, 2, 3], B_str = "1,2,3". B_str + "," + B_str = "1,2,3,1,2,3"
    const extended_B_str = B_str + ',' + B_str;
    
    return extended_B_str.includes(T_str);
}


function solve(nums) {
    const n = nums.length;
    
    // 1. Sort nums to get S
    const S = [...nums].sort((a, b) => a - b);

    // 2. Find all divisors k of n
    const divisors = [];
    for (let k = 1; k * k <= n; k++) {
        if (n % k === 0) {
            divisors.push(k);
            if (k * k !== n) {
                divisors.push(n / k);
            }
        }
    }
    
    let sum_k = 0;

    // 3. Loop through divisors k
    for (const k of divisors) {
        let is_sortable = true;
        
        // 4. Iterate over blocks
        const m = n / k;
        for (let i = 0; i < m; i++) {
            const start = i * k;
            const end = (i + 1) * k;
            
            // i. Define B_i (original block)
            const B_i = nums.slice(start, end);
            
            // ii. Define S_i (target block)
            const S_i = S.slice(start, end);
            
            // iii. Check rotation
            if (!isCyclicRotation(B_i, S_i)) {
                is_sortable = false;
                break;
            }
        }
        
        // 5. Accumulate result
        if (is_sortable) {
            sum_k += k;
        }
    }
    
    return sum_k;
}
